transition_constraints.phi_object_to_memory: allowed on wide family-conditioned overlap bands
transition_constraints.phi_object_to_disc: allowed only on narrow restricted overlap bands
transition_constraints.phi_object_to_relation: allowed on medium overlap bands anchored to family structure

theory_implications.core_statement: The object-disc overlap should be significantly narrower than the object-memory overlap.
theory_implications.why: This matches the repeated P3 bottleneck: memory compatibility is easier to preserve than discriminative transport.
theory_implications.atlas_link: Family-specific radii and intrusion gaps can be used as first-order proxies for overlap width.

verdict.core_answer: Restricted overlap maps can now be parameterized in a family-conditioned way rather than treated as globally uniform.
verdict.next_theory_target: use these overlap maps to drive a theory-guided exclusion loop on P3 transport/readout candidates
